title: Djurmo

'''Djurmo''' was a blasted [[Volcano/Legends|volcanic]] [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="GatORW" /> located in the [[Djurmo system/Legends|Djurmo system]], a part of the [[Strabin sector/Legends|Strabin sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Huralok]] [[Species/Legends|species]], members of which spoke the [[Huralok (language)|Huralok]] [[Language/Legends|language]] and became well-known during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] for [[Pirate/Legends|piracy]]. [[Redkihl Rokk]] was a Huralok pirate from Djurmo.<ref name="GatORW" />

Between [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]] and [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]], Djurmo was situated within the borders of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]],<ref name="EGTW" /> and at some point between [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]] and [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]], the Djurmo [[Star system/Legends|system]] was conquered by the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extragalactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] species as part of its [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="TEA" />

==Behind the scenes==
Djurmo was first mentioned in the [[2004]] [[Wizards of the Coast]] ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' sourcebook ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''. The book placed it both in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] and the Mid Rim.<ref name="GatORW" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently confirmed the Mid Rim placement and established that the Djurmo system, and therefore the world itself, was situated in grid square M-7.<ref name="TEA" />
